evals.prompt: Draft follow up for Omar Al-Rashidi, Head of Supply Chain at a major KSA retailer. I emailed him 5 days ago about our WMS automation platform and heard nothing back. My name is Khalid. He's based in Riyadh.
evals.expected_output: A follow-up email under 60 words referencing a KSA-specific regulatory hook (SASO/Saber or NTP), a specific anonymized social proof metric, a peer-to-peer warm tone, and a concrete day-specific CTA. Includes a subject line under 6 words and drafting notes.

assertions.name: word_count_under_60
assertions.description: The email body (between greeting and sign-off) must be strictly under 60 words

assertions.name: no_hollow_phrases
assertions.description: The message must not contain phrases like 'just following up', 'checking in', 'circling back', or apologies for emailing

assertions.name: gcc_regulatory_hook_present
assertions.description: The message must reference a KSA-specific regulatory or operational context (e.g., SASO, Saber, NTP, Vision 2030)

assertions.name: specific_metric_in_social_proof
assertions.description: The social proof must include a specific, measurable result (hours saved, percentage improvement, etc.)

assertions.name: day_specific_cta
assertions.description: The CTA must reference a specific day or time window, not a vague 'whenever works for you'

assertions.name: subject_line_6_words_max
assertions.description: Subject line must be 6 words or fewer

evals.prompt: Generate follow-up 2 for Sara Al-Mansoori, Operations Director at a UAE logistics company. We already sent follow-up 1 last week. She hasn't replied. Sender name: Rania. Company is based in Dubai.
evals.expected_output: A Follow-up 2 message using a DIFFERENT angle/hook from what would have been used in Follow-up 1. UAE-specific hook (Dubai Customs, Jafza, etc.), different social proof, same tone constraints (under 60 words, peer-to-peer, no hollow phrases). Different subject line pattern.

assertions.name: word_count_under_60
assertions.description: The email body must be strictly under 60 words

assertions.name: uae_specific_hook
assertions.description: The hook must be UAE-specific (e.g., Dubai Customs, Jebel Ali, Jafza, UAE e-commerce regulation)

assertions.name: follow_up_2_tone_distinction
assertions.description: Message acknowledges prior contact briefly but leads with a new angle or different value framing

assertions.name: no_hollow_phrases
assertions.description: No 'just following up', 'checking in', 'circling back', or apologetic language

assertions.name: day_specific_cta
assertions.description: CTA includes a named day or specific time window

evals.prompt: Sequence step 2 — send a break-up message to Ahmed Al-Kuwari at a Qatar procurement firm. We've tried twice already. His company deals with project logistics for infrastructure projects. Sender is Maya.
evals.expected_output: A Follow-up 3 (break-up) message that is short, warm, zero-pressure, references a Qatar-specific hook briefly, leaves the door open without chasing, and exits gracefully. Tone is distinctly lower-key than steps 1 and 2.

assertions.name: break_up_tone
assertions.description: Message signals it's the last outreach for now without being passive-aggressive or desperate

assertions.name: leaves_door_open
assertions.description: Message explicitly signals the prospect can reach out when timing is right, without pressuring

assertions.name: qatar_context_referenced
assertions.description: Message briefly mentions a Qatar-relevant context (Hamad Port, infrastructure projects, MOCI, etc.)

assertions.name: no_hollow_phrases
assertions.description: No apologetic filler or hollow phrases used
